source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 9.6.1 kill command (p. 163)

questions.q: Forcibly kill the process with PID 1664 using signal 9.
questions.why: kill -9 sends SIGKILL, which interrupts the process outright.

questions.q: What does the kill command actually do?
options: Sends a signal to a process (signals are how processes communicate)
Only works on your own shell
questions.why: kill sends a signal to a process; signals are the means of inter-process communication.

questions.q: Which signal number is SIGKILL, the forceful interrupt?
questions.why: Signal 9 is SIGKILL; 15 is SIGTERM (clean termination), 2 is SIGINT.

questions.q: Which signal is the CLEAN termination (and the default sent by kill)?
questions.why: SIGTERM (15) asks a process to terminate cleanly; it is the default when no signal is specified.

questions.q: Which pair of signals suspends and then resumes a process (the Ctrl+z effect and its reverse)?
questions.why: SIGSTOP (19) suspends a process (equivalent to Ctrl+z); SIGCONT (18) lets it continue.

questions.q: Show the complete list of signals the kill command understands (via the manual).
questions.why: The full signal list is in section 7 of the manual: man 7 signal.
